title.rendered: CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026: Check Revised Marks, Date, Direct Link

content.rendered:
The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) is gearing up to publish the CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026 shortly. Following the initial declaration of the Class 12 board exam results on May 13, 2026, students who felt their scores did not reflect their performance opted for the post-result verification process, which includes marks verification, obtaining photocopies of answer scripts, and final re-evaluation.

With university admissions and competitive counselling sessions moving forward rapidly, students are anxiously anticipating their updated marksheets. As soon as the board announces the results, candidates can verify their modified scores directly through the official CBSE online portals.

Below, you will find all the essential information, including expected release dates, direct links, step-by-step checking procedures, crucial deadlines, scorecard details, and a comprehensive FAQ section regarding the CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026.

Table of Contents

  • What If Marks Decrease After Re-evaluation?
  • Conclusion
  • FAQs

CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026 Latest Update

Candidates who submitted applications for marks verification, answer sheet photocopies, and subsequent re-evaluation are closely monitoring the internet for the CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026. According to the most recent information, the board is likely to publish these revised scores by the final week of June 2026.

Once officially declared, students can log onto the designated CBSE websites to access their updated performance records. The new scorecard will highlight any adjustments made during the rechecking phase. To ensure a smooth checking experience, students should have their roll number, school number, and admit card ID handy.

CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026: Overview & Official Dates

Before checking your revised marks, it is crucial to review the complete timeline and exam details. CBSE officially revised the dates for the verification and re-evaluation process this year. Below is the summarised overview along with the official schedule:

CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026 Direct Link

As soon as the results get the green light from the board, students can view their altered marks online. The direct links to check the revised scores will be activated on the following official platforms:

  • cbse.gov.in
  • results.cbse.nic.in
  • DigiLocker

How to Check Whether Your Marks Have Been Revised?

A common question among students is how to confirm if their scores actually shifted during the rechecking phase. Once CBSE makes the re-evaluation results live, you simply need to match your new online scorecard against your original May 13 marksheet. Note that any score reflected in this new result will be considered absolute and final.

  • Revised scores will be exclusively updated on the official result portal.
  • The modified marks will also be pushed to your DigiLocker account.
  • CBSE will dispatch a fresh physical marksheet if your total changes.
  • Cross-check the old and new scorecards side-by-side to spot subject-specific adjustments.
  • The post-re-evaluation marks are permanent and cannot be contested further.

Related Read: CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Result

How to Check CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026 Online?

Once the much-awaited results are out, downloading your updated scorecard is a quick process. Keep your admit card nearby to enter your details accurately.

Steps to Check the Result:

  1. Navigate to the official CBSE portal (cbse.gov.in or results.cbse.nic.in).
  2. Go to the “Results” section.
  3. Locate and click on the specific link for “CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026.”
  4. Type in your official Roll Number.
  5. Provide your School Number and Admit Card ID.
  6. Hit the “Submit” button.
  7. Your updated re-evaluation scorecard will be displayed.
  8. Download the document and print a copy for your college admission files.

Expected Time of CBSE 12th Re-evaluation Result 2026

While CBSE rarely announces an exact hour for result declarations beforehand, past patterns indicate that re-evaluation updates are typically pushed live during standard working hours. You can generally expect the link to go live:

  • Between 10:00 AM and 2:00 PM.
  • On a standard working day.
  • Directly on the main CBSE result websites.

Keep refreshing the official pages for the most accurate timing updates.

Details Mentioned on CBSE 12th Re-evaluation Scorecard 2026

Upon downloading your newly generated result, carefully scan the document to ensure all personal and academic details are accurate. If you spot a printing error, contact your school administration immediately. The scorecard will feature:

  • Student Name
  • Roll Number
  • School Number
  • Subject Names
  • Subject-wise Marks
  • Revised Marks (if applicable)
  • Total Marks
  • Result Status (Pass/Fail)
  • Division
  • Percentage

What Happens After the Re-evaluation Result is Declared?

Understanding the impact of the newly published result is vital. Once the re-evaluation scores are public, they officially override your previous marks for all future academic and admission requirements.

  • You must download the fresh scorecard right away and verify the details.
  • The new marks are absolute and binding.
  • Universities and colleges will evaluate your profile based on these updated scores.
  • A newly printed physical marksheet will be supplied to your school if your score has changed.
  • The board does not entertain any further appeals regarding evaluation.

For those who still did not secure passing marks after the recheck, you can look into the CBSE Class 12th Compartment Exam 2026 guidelines for another attempt.

What If Marks Increase After Re-evaluation?

The primary reason students opt for re-evaluation is the hope of gaining extra points. If the examiners find that you deserve more marks, CBSE will officially increase your score. These higher marks will then become your permanent academic record.

  • The increased marks will be visible on the result portal.
  • A new, updated hardcopy marksheet will be distributed.
  • The higher score stands as your final board result.
  • You can immediately use the digital printout for ongoing college counselling rounds.

What If Marks Decrease After Re-evaluation?

It is equally important to remember the risks involved: scores can be reduced if an over-evaluation is discovered during the review. This is why students are always advised to thoroughly analyse their requested answer sheet photocopies before applying for the final re-evaluation step.

  • Scores are subject to increase, decrease, or no change at all.
  • If marks are deducted, the lowered score becomes your final, permanent grade.
  • The updated digital record is considered the ultimate official score.
  • No subsequent requests to revert to the old score are permitted.

Conclusion

The CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026 is projected to be released by the concluding week of June 2026. Candidates who invested in the verification and rechecking phases should keep a close eye on the official CBSE portals for the imminent link activation.

Once the data is live, use your specific login credentials to pull up your updated scorecard and see if your totals have shifted. It is highly recommended to have your college application paperwork ready so you can seamlessly update your academic profile. Bookmark this article for real-time updates regarding the CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026, direct download links, and future board notifications!

  • CBSE Class 12th Result
  • CBSE 12th Compartment Exam
  • CBSE 12th Sikar Topper
  • Difference Between the CBSE and the RBSE Curriculum

FAQs

When will the CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026 be released?

The CBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation Result 2026 is anticipated to drop in the last week of June 2026.

Where can I check the CBSE 12th Re-evaluation Result 2026?

You can view your updated performance on the official portals: cbse.gov.in and results.cbse.nic.in.

What login credentials are required to check the re-evaluation result?

You must enter your Roll Number, School Number, and Admit Card ID to access the dashboard.

Can marks decrease after CBSE re-evaluation?

Yes. Following the recheck, your final marks have the potential to increase, decrease, or stay exactly the same.

How will I know if my marks have changed?

By comparing your newly downloaded online scorecard with the original marksheet you received in May.

Will CBSE issue a new marksheet after re-evaluation?

Yes. If your score undergoes any alteration (up or down), CBSE will issue a freshly printed marksheet.

What if there is no change in my marks?

Your initial May results remain legally valid, and no alterations will appear on your record.

Is the CBSE re-evaluation result final?

Yes. The scores granted after this phase are binding and final.

Is DigiLocker updated after CBSE re-evaluation?

Yes. Any revised scores are subsequently synced and updated on the DigiLocker platform.

Can I use the revised marksheet for college admission?

Absolutely. Universities and higher education institutes fully accept the updated marksheet for all admission-related procedures.

title.rendered: CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Result 2026: Check Date, Result Link, Latest Updates

content.rendered:
If you are one of the thousands of students who applied for the CBSE Class 10 revaluation this year, the wait is almost over. Following the declaration of the board results, many students opted for verification of marks, photocopies of answer sheets, and re-evaluation to ensure their performance was assessed accurately.

Now, students are eagerly waiting for the release of the revised scores. Here is everything you need to know about the CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Result 2026, including the expected result date, official websites, checking process, and important updates.

Table of Contents

Toggle
  • Latest Updates on CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Results 2026
  • CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Result 2026 Expected Date
  • Official Websites to Check CBSE Revaluation Result 2026
  • How to Check CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Result 2026 Online
    • Step 1: Visit the Official Website
    • Step 2: Click on the Revaluation Result Link
    • Step 3: Enter Required Details
    • Step 4: Submit Information
    • Step 5: Download the Result
  • Details Mentioned on CBSE Revaluation Result 2026
  • What Happens After the Revaluation Result is Declared?
    • Marks May Increase, Decrease, or Remain Unchanged
    • Issuance of Revised Marksheet
    • Final Decision of the Board
  • Important Note for Students
  • Conclusion
  • FAQs

Latest Updates on CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Results 2026

The revaluation and verification process for CBSE Class 10 has been completed, and students are now awaiting the publication of revised marks. While CBSE has not officially announced the exact result date, the updated scores are expected to be released by 3rd week of June on the official portals.

Students should regularly visit the official CBSE websites and avoid relying on unofficial reports or social media rumours regarding result announcements.

  • The CBSE class 10th or 12th revaluation application process has concluded.
  • Revised marks are expected to be released shortly.
  • Updated marksheets will be issued only if there is a change in marks.
  • Students can check results online using their board examination credentials.

CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Result 2026 Expected Date

One of the most common questions among students is when the revaluation result will be declared. Although CBSE has not released an official notification regarding the result date, previous years’ trends provide a reasonable estimate. The board generally publishes revised scores within a few weeks after the completion of the re-evaluation process.

Note: The expected result date is based on previous CBSE result trends. The board has not yet officially confirmed the exact release date of the CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Result 2026.

Official Websites to Check CBSE Revaluation Result 2026

Students should use only official CBSE platforms to access their revised scorecards. This helps prevent confusion and protects students from fake websites or misleading result links. Always keep your login credentials ready before checking the result online.

Official Websites:

  • cbseresults.nic.in
  • cbse.gov.in
  • results.cbse.nic.in
  • DigiLocker
  • UMANG App

How to Check CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Result 2026 Online

Once CBSE activates the result link, students can access their revised marks through a simple online process. Keeping your admit card nearby will make the process easier and faster. Follow these steps carefully to view and download your updated scorecard.

Step 1: Visit the Official Website

Open cbseresults.nic.in or cbse.gov.in.

Step 2: Click on the Revaluation Result Link

Look for the notification titled “CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Result 2026”.

Step 3: Enter Required Details

Provide:

  • Roll Number
  • School Number
  • Admit Card ID
  • Date of Birth

Step 4: Submit Information

Complete the security captcha and click on Submit.

Step 5: Download the Result

Your revised scorecard will appear on the screen. Download and save it for future reference.

Details Mentioned on CBSE Revaluation Result 2026

After downloading the revised scorecard, students should carefully verify all information mentioned in the result. Any discrepancy should be reported to the school authorities immediately. The revised result typically includes the following details:

  • Student Name
  • Roll Number
  • School Number
  • Subject-wise Marks
  • Revised Marks
  • Total Marks
  • Result Status
  • Examination Year
  • Board Name

What Happens After the Revaluation Result is Declared?

Many students assume that marks only increase after revaluation, but that is not always the case. The final outcome depends entirely on the review conducted by CBSE evaluators. Understanding the post-result process can help students make informed decisions regarding admissions and academic planning.

Marks May Increase, Decrease, or Remain Unchanged

After re-evaluation, marks can:

  • Increase
  • Decrease
  • Remain the same

The revised score becomes the final official score.

Issuance of Revised Marksheet

If marks are changed, CBSE issues an updated marksheet reflecting the revised scores.

Final Decision of the Board

The result released after re-evaluation is considered final. No further requests for mark changes are generally entertained by the board.

Important Note for Students

Students should remember that the expected dates mentioned in this article are based on previous trends and publicly available information. The final schedule depends entirely on CBSE’s official announcements.

For the latest updates, students are advised to regularly check official CBSE portals and their school notifications.

Conclusion

The CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Result 2026 is expected to be announced soon on the official CBSE portals. Students who applied for verification of marks or re-evaluation should keep their credentials ready and regularly monitor official updates.

Once released, the revised marks will become the final official score and will be reflected in the updated marksheet issued by CBSE. Students should rely only on official sources for accurate information and avoid unofficial result-related rumours circulating online.

FAQs

When will CBSE Class 10 Revaluation Result 2026 be released?

The result is expected to be announced between late June and July 2026, subject to official confirmation by CBSE.

Can marks decrease after revaluation?

Yes. Marks may increase, decrease, or remain unchanged after the re-evaluation process.

Where can I check the CBSE Revaluation Result 2026?

Students can check their revised scores on the official CBSE result portals, DigiLocker, and UMANG.

Is the revaluation result final?

Yes. The revised marks released by CBSE are considered final.

Will I receive a new marksheet if my marks change?

Yes. CBSE issues an updated marksheet whenever there is a change in marks after re-evaluation.

title.rendered: CBSE 2nd Board Exam Result 2026: Check Date, How to Check, Direct Link, and Live Updates

content.rendered:
CBSE Class 10 and Class 12 Session 2 examinations have concluded successfully, and now lakhs of students are eagerly waiting for the CBSE 2nd Board Exam Result 2026. Under CBSE’s new dual-board examination system, students were given a second opportunity to improve their scores without losing an academic year.

While CBSE has not officially announced the Session 2 result date yet, the results are expected in June 2026 after the completion of the evaluation process. Here is everything you need to know about the CBSE Session 2 Result 2026, including the expected release date, direct result link, DigiLocker marksheet, best-of-two scoring system, and post-result process.

Table of Contents

Toggle
  • CBSE 2nd Board Exam Result 2026 Date
  • Official Websites to Check Your Session 2 Scores
  • Step-by-Step Guide to Download Your CBSE 2nd Board Marksheet
  • Details Mentioned on CBSE 2nd Board Marksheet 2026
  • Understanding the “Best of Two” Scoring System
  • What to Do After the Results Are Declared?
  • Conclusion
  • FAQs

CBSE 2nd Board Exam Result 2026 Date

The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) successfully conducted the Session 2 Board Examinations for Class 10 and Class 12 in May 2026. Students who appeared for the second board exam are now eagerly waiting for the declaration of the CBSE 2nd Board Exam Result 2026. Although CBSE has not officially confirmed the result date yet, based on the completion of the evaluation process and recent board updates, the results are expected to be released in the fourth week of June 2026. Once announced, students will be able to check their scorecards online through the official CBSE result portals using their Roll Number, School Number, and Admit Card ID.

CBSE Session 2 Result 2026 Important Dates

Official Websites to Check Your Session 2 Scores

During the result declaration day, heavy traffic can sometimes cause server crashes on the main portal, leading to unnecessary panic. To ensure students do not face any hassle while checking their CBSE 2nd Board Exam Result 2026, the board makes the scorecards available across multiple digital platforms. Keep your admit card details handy before visiting these official channels.

You can verify your marks securely through the following authorised platforms:

  • cbseresults.nic.in: The primary and most direct portal for all CBSE results.
  • results.cbse.nic.in: The alternative official result hosting website.
  • cbse.gov.in: The central academic website of the board.
  • DigiLocker App/Website: A highly recommended government platform to access your digital marksheet, passing certificate, and migration certificate.
  • UMANG App: A unified mobile application where students can access their scorecards seamlessly via their smartphones.

Step-by-Step Guide to Download Your CBSE 2nd Board Marksheet

Navigating through the result portals can sometimes be confusing, especially when you are anxious and eager to see your scores. We have put together a simple, hassle-free guide to help you access your CBSE Session 2 results quickly. Follow these exact steps to download your digital marksheet without any technical interruptions.

  1. Visit the Official Portal: Open your web browser and go to cbseresults.nic.in.
  2. Locate the Result Link: On the homepage, look for the flashing link that reads “Secondary School Examination (Class X) Session 2 Results 2026” or “Senior School Certificate Examination (Class XII) Session 2 Results 2026”.
  3. Enter Your Credentials: You will be directed to a login window. Enter your Roll Number, School Number, and Admit Card ID exactly as they appear on your hall ticket.
  4. Submit and View: Click on the “Submit” button. Your detailed subject-wise scorecard will instantly appear on the screen.
  5. Download and Print: Thoroughly check your personal details and marks. Click on the download icon and take a printout for your immediate academic records until the physical marksheet arrives.

Details Mentioned on CBSE 2nd Board Marksheet 2026

After downloading the CBSE Session 2 result, students should carefully verify all information mentioned on the marksheet. In case of any discrepancy, they should immediately contact their school authorities or CBSE officials for correction.

  • Student Name
  • Roll Number
  • School Name
  • Subject Names
  • Subject-wise Marks
  • Grades
  • Result Status
  • Date of Birth

Understanding the “Best of Two” Scoring System

One of the biggest advantages of the new National Education Policy (NEP) guidelines is the introduction of this flexible dual-exam system. This policy aims to radically reduce student stress by offering a chance to improve scores without wasting an entire academic year. Here is exactly how the board will calculate your final marks using the two exam sessions.

  • Independent Subject Evaluation: The system evaluates your performance on a subject-by-subject basis, rather than taking an overall average of both exams.
  • Highest Score Retained: If you scored 85 in Mathematics in Session 1, and 92 in Session 2, your final official scorecard will reflect the 92.
  • No Penalty for Dropping Marks: If your performance drops in the second session (e.g., scoring 80 in Science in Session 2 after scoring 88 in Session 1), the board will automatically retain the higher Session 1 score (88).
  • Combined Marksheet: The final marksheet issued via DigiLocker and your school will automatically consolidate your highest scores from both attempts, presenting a single, optimised final grade.

What to Do After the Results Are Declared?

Once you have successfully downloaded your CBSE 2nd Board Exam Result 2026, it is time to plan your next academic moves. Whether you are thrilled with your score or feel there is still room for improvement, there are clear protocols and options in place. Take a deep breath and review the following steps available to you.

  • Collect Original Documents: The online printout is only valid for immediate admission processes. You must collect your physical, stamped marksheet and passing certificate from your respective school once they notify you.
  • Apply for Re-evaluation: If you are confident that your paper was not evaluated correctly, CBSE will open a brief window for verification of marks, obtaining photocopies of answer sheets, and re-evaluation. Keep an eye on cbse.gov.in for these dates.
  • Finalise Admissions: Class 12 students should immediately use their updated scores for CUET counselling, JEE/NEET document verification, or direct college admissions. Class 10 students can finalise their stream selection (Science, Commerce, or Arts) based on their improved results.

Conclusion

The CBSE 2nd Board Exam Result 2026 will play an important role for students looking to improve their academic performance under the new dual-board examination system. Once declared, students can access their Class 10 and Class 12 Session 2 scorecards through the official CBSE result portals, DigiLocker, and UMANG.

Students should keep their Roll Number, School Number, and Admit Card ID ready and regularly check official CBSE notifications for the latest updates. The best-of-two scoring policy ensures that students receive the highest marks obtained across both attempts, making the system more flexible and student-friendly.

FAQs

When will the CBSE 2nd Board Exam Result 2026 be released?

The CBSE Session 2 Result 2026 is expected to be released between 24 June and 28 June 2026. However, students should wait for the official confirmation from CBSE.

Will CBSE issue a new marksheet after Session 2?

Yes, CBSE is expected to provide a consolidated marksheet containing the highest marks obtained across both sessions.

Where can I check the CBSE Session 2 Result 2026?

Students can check their results through the official website, cbseresults.nic.in, results.cbse.nic.in, DigiLocker, and the UMANG app.

What credentials are required to check the CBSE 2nd Board Result 2026?

Students need their Roll Number, School Number, and Admit Card ID to access their results online.

Will CBSE consider the higher score from both board exam sessions?

Yes. Under the best-of-two system, CBSE will retain the highest marks obtained by a student in each subject across both sessions.

Can I apply for re-evaluation after the CBSE Session 2 result is declared?

Yes. CBSE will provide facilities for verification of marks, obtaining answer sheet photocopies, and re-evaluation after the result declaration.

Will DigiLocker provide the official CBSE marksheet?

Yes. Students can download their digitally verified marksheet, passing certificate, and other academic documents through DigiLocker.

What are the passing marks for CBSE Board Exams 2026?

Students must secure at least 33% marks in each subject to pass the CBSE Board Examination 2026.

Will CBSE release separate marksheets for Session 1 and Session 2?

No. CBSE is expected to issue a consolidated final marksheet that reflects the best score obtained in each subject.

What should I do if the CBSE result website is not opening?

Due to heavy traffic, students can use alternative platforms such as DigiLocker, UMANG, or the secondary CBSE result portal to check their scores.

When will the re-evaluation application process start?

The re-evaluation and verification process is expected to begin within a few days after the declaration of the CBSE Session 2 Result 2026.

title.rendered: CBSE Rechecking 2026 Class 10 & 12: Complete Guide to Verification, Revaluation & Answer Sheet Copy

content.rendered:
After the declaration of CBSE Class 10 and 12 results 2026, students who are not satisfied with their marks can apply for Verification of Marks, Photocopy of Answer Sheet, and Revaluation.

Every year, the board provides the rechecking facility to students who appear in the annual board examination. The application window for CBSE Rechecking for Class 10 and Class 12 will open soon, as the CBSE Board Results 2026 for Class 10th and 12th are announced. Based on the CBSE report, in 2025, thousands of students from classes 10 and 12 applied for the CBSE rechecking to improve their marks or verify calculation errors.

This article provides complete information on CBSE rechecking, including eligibility, rules, types of rechecking, important dates, application fee, and a step-by-step application process for both class 10 and class 12.

Latest Update (2 June 2026): CBSE has activated the portal for Verification of Issues observed in supplied scanned copies of answer books and Re-evaluation of Answers. Students who have already received the scanned copy of their evaluated answer book can apply online from 2 June 2026 to 6 June 2026 (Midnight) through the official CBSE portal.

Table of Contents

Toggle
  • What is CBSE Copy Rechecking for Class 10th and 12th?
  • CBSE Rechecking Eligibility 2026
  • CBSE Rechecking Fees 2026 (Latest Official Update)
  • Important Dates for CBSE Rechecking for Class 10 and 12
  • CBSE Rechecking Process 2026
  • CBSE Rechecking Result 2026 Download for Class 10 & 12
  • What to do after CBSE Rechecking Result 2026?
  • Conclusion
  • FAQs

What is CBSE Copy Rechecking for Class 10th and 12th?

The CBSE rechecking is a post-result process that allows students to challenge their allotted marks in class 10 and 12 answer books. The primary purpose of the CBSE rechecking is to verify the accuracy of the marks awarded in the answer sheet.

Students can also ask for a scanned copy of the answer sheet to apply for re-evaluation to correct mistakes in a particular answer. The following are the key importance of the CBSE Post Result Verification.

  • Rectifies Error: CBSE ensures the major corrections like calculation mistakes, totalling errors, and questions that were missed while making the final result.
  • Ensure the Result Accuracy: the board provides a safety net to students to ensure marks awarded in the exam are based on their quality answers, not clerical errors.
  • Transparency: Students can request a photocopy of the answer sheet to review the marks and feel confident while applying for the re-evaluation/ rechecking.
  • Improvement in Score: If any error found while rechecking, marks can be increased or decreased, which impacts the overall result.

Also Read:- CBSE Class 12th Result 2026

CBSE Rechecking Eligibility 2026

If you are applying for the CBSE Rechecking 2026, clearly check out the eligibility set for both class 10 and class 12. If you want a smooth experience while submitting the rechecking form, please confirm your eligibility by following the points given below.

  • Only those students who appeared in the class 10 or 12 board examinations in the current session will be eligible for the CBSE Rechecking/ Re-evaluation/ verification of marks.
  • The official CBSE website will be the only source to submit the rechecking form online.
  • No offline application form will be accepted for CBSE rechecking.
  • The application window for the rechecking or re-evaluation will be opened after the declaration of the results.
  • Within the given deadline, eligible students can submit the application form.
  • The rechecking facility will be available only for the theory exam for classes 10 & 12.
  • A non-refundable application fee is required to complete the process.

CBSE Rechecking Fees 2026 (Latest Official Update)

The Central Board of Secondary Education set a specific fee for each stage. The expected CBSE Rechecking/ Re-Evaluation/ Verification of Marks fee for class 10 and class 12 is as shown in the table below. The fees are non-refundable in most cases. Latest CBSE Update: As per the official CBSE press release dated 2 June 2026, the fee for Verification of Issues in supplied scanned copies of answer books is ₹100 per answer book, while the fee for Re-evaluation is ₹25 per question. Students can submit requests only through the online portal.

Important Dates for CBSE Rechecking for Class 10 and 12

CBSE will soon begin the rechecking/revaluation/verification of Class 10 and 12 marks. This process usually begins immediately after the results are announced. If you’re here to find out the CBSE Date Sheet 2026 for rechecking/revaluation/verification of marks, carefully review the points below.

Latest Official Update: The CBSE portal for Verification of Issues and Re-evaluation has been opened from 2 June 2026 to 6 June 2026 for students who have already obtained the scanned copy of their evaluated answer books.

CBSE Rechecking Process 2026

The application window will open 4 days after the results are declared in classes 10 and 12. In case students are not happy with the mark’s allocation and feel that either some questions were not checked or calculation mistake, they can raise an application for rechecking. Mainly, the process includes 3 different stages, and each has its own eligibility to provide a transparent and fair result. Detailed explanation is as follows.

01. Request a Photocopy of the Answer Sheet

Students have to first request a scanned photocopy of the answer sheet from the board to evaluate their allotted marks. Through this, students can review their answers and allotted marks accordingly. Here are some steps to obtain a scanned copy of the answer sheet.

  • Visit the official website of CBSE, i.e. cbseit.in
  • Find the link to “Apply for Answer Book”.
  • Click on the details and fill in the required details.
  • Also, pay the relevant fee in online mode.
  • Submit the request and download the copy available on the website.
  • You can also take a printout of it for further use.

02. Verification of Marks (CBSE Re-Totalling)

Once you download the CBSE scanned answer book copy, check your marks carefully, whether all the marks given in the answer sheet are correct or not, and whether the total is also calculated correctly or not. Follow the steps below if you find any discrepancy in the marks.

  • First, visit the official CBSE website, i.e. cbseit.in
  • Search for “Verification of Marks” in the “Examination” Section.
  • Click on the link and fill in the required details.
  • Select the subject you want to apply for.
  • Pay the required fee and click on submit.
  • Wait for the final result after verification of marks.

03. CBSE Re-Evaluation of Answers

If students are not satisfied with the evaluation of specific answers after reviewing the scanned copy of their answer book, they can apply for re-evaluation. Students must provide the question number and page number while submitting the application. Re-evaluation is available only after obtaining the scanned copy of the evaluated answer book.

  • Marks may be increased,
  • Marks may be decreased,
  • Marks may remain unchanged, etc.

04. Verification of Issues in Scanned Copy of Answer Book

Students who have already obtained the scanned copy of their evaluated answer books can apply for the resolution of issues observed in the supplied answer book.

Students can report:

  • Evaluation against a different question paper set
  • Missing pages
  • Missing supplementary sheets
  • Missing maps or graphs
  • Blurred or unreadable pages
  • The incorrect answer book was uploaded
  • Evaluation against a different question paper set

CBSE Rechecking Result 2026 Download for Class 10 & 12

After the rechecking requests are made by the respective students, CBSE will release the CBSE rechecking results for classes 10 and 12. The result is expected to be announced in June or July 2026. The following steps you can use to get the result online.

  • Navigate to the official CBSE Portal, i.e. cbse.gov.in
  • Find the link to download “Rechecking Result” on the home page.
  • Click on the link if available there.
  • Provide the essential details, like roll number and admit card ID, to access the result.
  • Now click on the link and wait for the result.
  • Download and take a printout of the result for further use.

NOTE: If there are any changes to students’ results, they can only check the updated results on the official website. However, those whose results have not changed will not be able to see the updated results.

What to do after CBSE Rechecking Result 2026?

If your result is positive, as you wish, you can get enrolled in the next class. However, if the result remains the same after rechecking or re-evaluation, consider taking these further steps.

  • Such students can appear for compartment or supplementary exams.
  • For supplementary exams, if you are in Class 10 or 12, you can only apply for one or two subjects.
  • Check the compartment exam eligibility before submitting the application form.
  • Regular students will need to contact their school to submit the application form.
  • Private students can submit the form online through the official CBSE portal.
  • While submitting the CBSE Compartment Form 2026, you must provide the necessary details, documents and the application fee.
  • The eligibility and application fees for both Class 10 and Class 12 are different. Therefore, check the details carefully to avoid any rejection.
  • In case of any rejection, students will not be given any prior notice from the Board.

CBSE has introduced an additional facility called Verification of Issues in Scanned Answer Books. Through this process, students can report missing pages, missing supplementary sheets, blurred scans, missing graphs/maps, or any discrepancy found in the supplied scanned copy before proceeding with re-evaluation.

Conclusion

CBSE has made the rechecking process much more affordable in 2026 by reducing the fees significantly. If you feel there is any mistake in your marks, now is the right time to apply; the cost is very low, and there is a full refund policy if the marks increase.
Always apply only through the official CBSE website (cbse.gov.in) and keep checking for the latest updates.

FAQs

Who can apply for CBSE rechecking in 2026?

Only students who appeared in the main board exam can apply for rechecking/re-evaluation/verification of marks.

What is Verification of Issues in Scanned Answer Books?

Students who receive scanned copies of their evaluated answer books can report issues such as missing pages, blurred pages, missing supplementary sheets, missing maps/graphs, incorrect answer books, or evaluation against a different question paper set through the CBSE verification portal.

What is the CBSE paper rechecking process?

This process is online, and students can only use it to evaluate any marking errors for theory papers.

Who can obtain a scanned copy of the CBSE answer book?

Students who appeared in CBSE Class 10 or Class 12 Board Exams and apply through the official CBSE portal can obtain the scanned copy of their evaluated answer book. Only students who have received the scanned copy are eligible to apply for Verification of Issues and Re-evaluation.

Does rechecking increase marks in Class 10 or 12?

The increase or decrease in marks will depend on the student’s original answer sheet. Therefore, carefully follow the process to correct any errors in the answer book.

Which is better, rechecking or re-evaluation?

Both are used for different purposes to correct mistakes in answer sheets. Rechecking is primarily used to ensure all questions are marked with no totalling errors, while re-evaluation is used for detailed re-examination of a specific answer.

How much does CBSE charge for rechecking/ re-evaluation/ verification of marks?

After the latest update on 17 May 2026:
Scanned Photocopy: ₹100 per subject
Verification of Marks: ₹100 per subject
Revaluation: ₹25 per question
Full refund if marks increase after revaluation.

What is the advantage of CBSE rechecking?

The advantage of CBSE rechecking is that any mistakes in the answer book are corrected. This may increase the marks and change the overall result of class 10 and 12 students.

title.rendered: RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 Out: Check Name Wise & Roll Number Wise

content.rendered:
The Board of Secondary Education, Rajasthan (RBSE) has officially declared the RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026. Students who appeared in the Class 12 supplementary examinations can now check their results online through the official website. Whether you want to check the result roll number-wise or explore name-wise result options, this guide provides direct result links, marksheet download information, and important post-result details.

This blog is specially written for you. Here you will get complete information about the Rajasthan Board 12th Supplementary Result 2026 in simple and easy language. Whether you want to check your result name-wise or roll number-wise, we have covered everything step by step. So stay with us till the end and bookmark this page for the latest updates.

Table of Contents

Toggle
  • RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 Direct Link
  • How to Check RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 Online (Roll Number Wise)
  • How to Check RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 Name Wise?
  • RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 Marksheet Download
  • What to Do After Checking Your RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026?
  • Important Tips for Students
  • Conclusion
  • FAQs

RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 Direct Link

RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 has been officially released by the Rajasthan Board. Students can check their results online using their roll number through the official result portal. Keep your roll number ready before accessing the result page.

Direct Result Links:

  • https://rajeduboard.rajasthan.gov.in/
  • https://rajresults.nic.in/
  • https://rajeduboard.rajasthan.gov.in/RESULT2026/SEV/Roll_Input.htm

Students should use only the official Rajasthan Board websites to check their results and download the provisional marksheet. The original marksheet will be provided through the respective schools after the result declaration process is completed.

RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 Direct Link Status: Active Now

How to Check RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 Online (Roll Number Wise)

Students can check their RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 online through the official Rajasthan Board website using their roll number.

  1. Visit the official website:- rajeduboard.rajasthan.gov.in.
  2. On the homepage, click on “RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026” available on the homepage.
  3. Click on the link.
  4. Enter your 9-digit Roll Number.
  5. Fill in other details if asked (like Mother’s Name or Date of Birth).
  6. Click on Submit or View Result.
  7. Your result will appear on the screen. Download and take a printout for future use.

How to Check RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 Name Wise?

Although RBSE primarily provides results through the roll number system, some trusted educational portals may offer a name-wise search facility after the result declaration. Students should verify their result through the official website for complete accuracy.

To make the process simple, we have explained the complete step-by-step method below for checking the Rajasthan Board 12th Supplementary Result 2026 name-wise without any confusion.

  1. Go to rajresults.nic.in or trusted portals like rajasthan.indiaresults.com.
  2. Select RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026.
  3. Choose the option “Search by Name” (if available).
  4. Enter your Full Name as per the admit card.
  5. Select your stream (Science, Commerce or Arts) and district if required.
  6. Click on Search.
  7. Your result will be displayed along with your roll number and marks.

Note: Official websites mostly work on roll number, but name-wise search is also made available on many educational portals after the result is declared.

RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 Marksheet Download

After checking the result, students can download the provisional RBSE 12th Supplementary Marksheet 2026 from the official website. The original marksheet will be distributed through schools later. Students should carefully verify their name, roll number, subject-wise marks, percentage, and qualifying status after downloading the marksheet.

Below are some important points related to the RBSE 12th Supplementary Marksheet 2026 that every student should know:

  • The online marksheet will be provisional in nature.
  • Students can download it from the official result portal after the result declaration.
  • The original marksheet will be provided by the respective schools.
  • Schools may distribute the marksheet a few days after the result announcement.
  • Students should collect the original marksheet on time for future admission and documentation purposes.
  • Always check details like name, roll number, subject-wise marks, and result status carefully.

What to Do After Checking Your RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026?

After checking the RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026, students should review their marks and plan their next academic step accordingly.

Students should also stay updated about important processes like original marksheet collection, revaluation, rechecking applications, and future examination opportunities. Below, we have explained the important options and next steps students can take after checking their Rajasthan Board 12th Supplementary Result 2026.

  • If you have passed → Congratulations. Collect your original marksheet from school and start preparing for further studies or competitive exams. Students who are also preparing for compartment exams in other boards can check the details of the CBSE 12th Compartment Exam 2026.
  • If you still need improvement in any subject, → You can appear for the next main examination in 2027.
  • For revaluation or rechecking → The board usually opens the revaluation window within 7-10 days after the result. Keep checking the official website.
  • Students who have passed can proceed with college admissions, professional courses, diploma programs, or competitive exam preparation.

Important Tips for Students

Students should save a copy of the online marksheet and collect the original marksheet from their school when available. In case of any discrepancy, contact school authorities immediately.

To help students avoid confusion and save time, we have shared some important tips below that every Rajasthan Board 12th student should follow:

  • Keep your roll number and admit card ready.
  • Use only official websites to avoid fake links.
  • Take a screenshot or print your result immediately after checking.
  • Share this blog with your friends who are also waiting for the Rajasthan Board 12th Supplementary Result 2026.

Conclusion

RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 has been officially declared by the Rajasthan Board. Students who appeared in the supplementary examinations can now check their results online using their roll number and download the provisional marksheet. Candidates who have successfully cleared the examination can continue with higher education, college admissions, and future career plans without losing an academic year.

Till then, stay positive and keep preparing for your bright future. All the very best to all the students. If you have any questions, feel free to ask in the comment box below. We will reply as soon as possible. Bookmark this page for instant updates on RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 Name Wise and Roll Number Wise.

Also Read:-

  • RBSE 10th and 12th Class Board Exam Dates
  • Top 10 Schools in Rajasthan 2026
  • Top 5 RBSE Schools in Sikar
  • RBSE 10th Result Sikar Toppers 2026

FAQs

Has RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 been released?

Yes, RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 has been officially declared by the Rajasthan Board.

Where can I check the RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026?

Students can check their result on:
rajeduboard.rajasthan.gov.in
rajresults.nic.in

Can I check the RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026 name-wise?

Yes, students may also check the result name-wise through educational result portals if the option is available after the result declaration.

What details are required to check the RBSE 12th Supplementary Result 2026?

Students mainly need their roll number to check the result online. Some portals may also ask for the date of birth or the mother’s name.

What should I do if I forget my roll number?

If you forget your roll number, you can try checking the result through the name-wise search option available on some result websites.

What is the official website of RBSE?

The official website of the Rajasthan Board is rajeduboard.rajasthan.gov.in.

Can I download my RBSE 12th Supplementary marksheet online?

Yes, students can download the provisional marksheet online after the result declaration.

When will the original marksheet be available?

The original marksheet will be distributed through schools a few days after the result announcement.

What should I do if there is an error in my marksheet?

Students should immediately contact their school authorities or the Rajasthan Board office for correction.

Can I apply for revaluation or rechecking?

Yes, RBSE usually opens the revaluation and rechecking process within a few days after the result declaration.
